California food safety regulation restaurants new rule

plan.rationale: Focus on California wage and food-safety obligations that hit a multi-site fast-casual restaurant employer most directly.

new_this_week.title: New food-handler certification rule (30-day deadline)
new_this_week.plain_english: CDPH now requires every food handler to complete updated certification within 30 days of hire. With three locations and regular hourly turnover, you need a tracking process so new hires are certified on time.
new_this_week.recommended_action: Add certification to onboarding, track completion dates per employee, and verify all current staff hold a valid certificate.

full_watchlist.title: California fast-food minimum wage rises to $20.50/hour
full_watchlist.plain_english: California's Fast Food Council raised the minimum wage for covered fast-food workers to $20.50/hour. As a Bay Area fast-casual operator with ~40 hourly staff, your payroll costs will rise next quarter and you must update pay rates and posted wage notices.
full_watchlist.recommended_action: Recalculate labor budgets at $20.50/hour, update payroll before the effective date, and post the new wage notice at each location.

full_watchlist.title: New food-handler certification rule (30-day deadline)
full_watchlist.plain_english: CDPH now requires every food handler to complete updated certification within 30 days of hire. With three locations and regular hourly turnover, you need a tracking process so new hires are certified on time.
full_watchlist.recommended_action: Add certification to onboarding, track completion dates per employee, and verify all current staff hold a valid certificate.

full_watchlist.title: Updated wage-theft recordkeeping guidance
full_watchlist.plain_english: DIR clarified employer recordkeeping obligations around wages and hours. You should confirm your time-tracking records meet the clarified standard.
full_watchlist.recommended_action: Audit time and payroll records against the updated guidance.
